title: Fermi Energy
summary: The Fermi energy is the reference energy that determines which band crossings control low-energy excitations; a crossing away from it does not automatically define a Weyl semimetal phase.
assumptions: The filling and chemical potential are specified.
regime: Band structures near half-filling or another chosen filling.
scope: Lecture One criterion for which crossings matter physically.

source_anchors.notes: Lecture One distinguishes topological crossings from low-energy physical relevance at the Fermi level.

representation: Energy reference that sets the active low-energy degrees of freedom.
failure_modes: A material can host Weyl-like crossings but still not realize a clean Weyl semimetal if the crossing lies far from E_F.
retrieval_hints: Use when deciding whether a crossing is physically relevant at low energy.
